fields.description:
## Context
For high assurance, a developer routes the gate to a trusted rebar-mcp server that runs the
verifier and signs. Integrity requires the server to fetch authoritative state itself (never client
inputs). Includes the AWS deployment (IaC declaration; actual provisioning is operator work).

**AWS infra decisions (from the epic's design resolution).** The signing key is **Ed25519**, signed
via **SSHSIG** by the server itself — NOT AWS KMS managed signing (AWS KMS asymmetric SIGN_VERIFY
supports only ECDSA/RSA, not Ed25519). Concretely:
- **Signing key storage:** the Ed25519 private key material lives in **AWS Secrets Manager**,
  encrypted at rest by a **KMS CMK**; the server reads it at signing time and runs SSHSIG. "KMS"
  is envelope-encryption of the secret, not the signing operation.
- **Endpoint authentication:** **AWS IAM (SigV4-signed requests) restricted to a named admin
  role**, over TLS. (This is the human/admin authentication requirement.)
- **Least-privilege IAM:** the server's execution role may read only the one signing secret + the
  repo (review remote) at the requested commit; nothing else.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The trusted rebar-mcp path fetches ticket state + code-at-commit from authoritative remotes
      itself and runs the gate on that; a test asserts a client-supplied doctored input does NOT
      change the signed verdict.
- [ ] The AWS MCP-server IaC declares: (a) endpoint authentication via AWS IAM SigV4 restricted to
      a named admin role over TLS; (b) least-privilege IAM for the signing-secret read + repo read;
      (c) the Ed25519 signing key material stored in AWS Secrets Manager (KMS-CMK-encrypted at
      rest), with the server performing SSHSIG signing itself — NOT AWS KMS managed signing.
- [ ] The default local path stays offline and never wedges an op (test/asserted).
